text.title: voice-agents Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for voice-agents as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using GPT-4o Realtime API (speech-to-speech) expecting tool-call support during streaming.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: GPT-4o Realtime API has no tool-call support during streaming as of 2026. The model must finish speaking before tools execute, adding a full turn of latency. Use STT → LLM → TTS pipeline for tool-heavy voice agents.

failure-mode.description: Not explicitly forbidding markdown in the voice agent system prompt.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: LLMs generate markdown formatting by default even when not instructed to. Without an explicit prohibition, roughly 20-30% of responses will contain formatting characters that TTS reads as literal text, breaking the natural conversation flow.

failure-mode.description: Autodialing for outbound campaigns without TCPA/GDPR consent workflow.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: TCPA (US) fines are $500-$1,500 per unconsented call. GDPR consent is required for EU citizens. Implement explicit consent collection and storage before any outbound campaign, and verify consent records are accessible to the agent for audits.
